CONTENT.Club career: Sow made his professional debut for Saint-Étienne on 3 October 2020, in a Ligue 1 game against Lens, replacing Maxence Rivera in the 16th minute, after the early expulsion of Timothée Kolodziejczak.
CONTENT.Career statistics: As of match played 21 April 2024As of matches played on 23 January 2024Scores and results list Sow's goal tally first.
CONTENT.International career: On 10 October 2020, Sow made his international debut for Guinea in a friendly game against Cape Verde, entering as a substitute and proving to be instrumental in his side 2–1 win by making the assist for Yady Bangoura's decisive goal.

SUMMARY: Saïdou Sow (born 4 July 2002) is a Guinean professional footballer who plays as a defender for  Ligue 1 club Strasbourg and the Guinea national team.
